title: Accessing Devices for rootless container
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Running deconz in a rootless podman container will lead to missing gateway firmware info / Zigbee channel not being shown in web-gui.
Running the same container as root will work flawless.

Using: ConBeeII

FirewallD and SELinux have been disabled for testing.



<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.

User has been added to dialout group do access device
`sudo usermod -a -G dialout $USER`


2.


Podman container settings
```
podman run -d \
    --name=deconz \
    --restart=always \
    -v /etc/localtime:/etc/localtime:ro \
    -v /home/username/deconz:/root/.local/share/dresden-elektronik/deCONZ:Z \
    --device=/dev/ttyACM0 \
    -e DECONZ_WEB_PORT=40850 \
    -e DECONZ_WS_PORT=40860 \
    -e DEBUG_INFO=1 \
    -e DEBUG_APS=0 \
    -e DEBUG_ZCL=0 \
    -e DEBUG_ZDP=0 \
    -e DEBUG_OTAU=0 \
    -p 40850:40850 \
    -p 40860:40860 \
    marthoc/deconz
```

3.

start podman container rootless


**Describe the results you received:**
Web ui wont show firmware/device information. Zigbee channel not shown and cant be changed.

**Describe the results you expected:**
Same behavior root/rootless container startup

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.6.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.1
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64

```

comments.body:
Does your user have permission to access `/dev/ttyACM0`? That's likely the issue here. You'll need to have permission not tied to a group, as well, as Podman drops supplemental groups in containers by default (though I believe we added a way around that recently...)

@giuseppe How do we invoke Podman to not drop supplemental groups when `crun` is in use, and did that make it into F31 yet?

You're right i only added the user to the group dialout.
Now i added the user directly to the device itself and that solved the problem.
SELinux did block the access however. Seems like i haven't noticed that before while testing it.

Perhaps that would be an helpfull entry to the readme in the section where the dialout group is mentioned.

Thank you very much for your quick support! This helped alot!
Please open a PR with the fixed wording.
